description: Database systems internals - storage, indexing, transactions, query processing.

facts.context: B+ trees store actual data records only in:
facts.truth: leaf nodes (internal nodes are index only)
distractors: all nodes equally distributed throughout the tree structure
the root node exclusively for fastest access times
randomly selected internal nodes for load balancing

facts.context: MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control) handles concurrent access by:
facts.truth: keeping multiple versions of each row
distractors: locking all rows during the entire transaction duration
serializing all transactions to execute one at a time
copying the entire table for each active transaction

facts.truth: logging changes before applying to data pages
distractors: writing all data pages to disk synchronously immediately
keeping all database data in memory only never persisting
replicating to backup server before committing transaction
read-heavy random access patterns with low latency
equal read and write performance in all cases
minimal memory usage at the cost of slower writes

facts.truth: I/O, CPU, and memory costs for execution plans
distractors: only wall-clock execution time in milliseconds
network latency between distributed database nodes
user-defined preference rankings for query results

facts.truth: appear to execute serially even if concurrent
distractors: complete in the minimum possible time always
can read uncommitted data from other transactions
automatically retry on any type of failure

facts.context: Database checkpointing reduces recovery time by:
facts.truth: persisting dirty pages limiting log replay
distractors: keeping duplicate copies of all data permanently
disabling writes during the checkpoint operation
compressing the transaction log to save space

facts.context: Hash join builds a hash table on:
facts.truth: the smaller relation for memory efficiency
distractors: the larger relation to maximize hash table utilization
both relations simultaneously for parallel execution
neither relation since sorting is used instead

facts.truth: physical storage order of table rows
distractors: only logical ordering for query results sorting
which columns are included in query projections
foreign key relationships between tables

facts.truth: no new locks acquired after any lock released
distractors: all locks acquired and released simultaneously
locks held for exactly two time units each
only read locks during the first phase of execution

facts.context: Database buffer pool eviction typically uses:
distractors: random eviction for simplicity and fairness
FIFO eviction based on page load time only
always evicting the largest pages first

facts.context: Selectivity estimation in query optimization predicts:
facts.truth: fraction of rows matching a predicate
distractors: which indexes are available for the query
total storage space required for results
optimal number of parallel workers needed
